title: chaptalia flavicans
intro: Chaptalia flavicans, also known as the yellow daisy or yerba de la reina, is a plant species in the daisy family that is native to Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Chaptalia flavicans include the potential treatment of various conditions, such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its immunomodulatory and antioxidant properties. Chaptalia flavicans can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms.
